items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: water activity in food science.
items.a: measure of free water available for microbes
items.explanation: Core explanation: measure of free water available for microbes.

items.q: In food science, what best defines 'water activity in food science'?
items.a: measure of free water available for microbes
options: measure of free water available for microbes
stabilize cocoa butter crystals for gloss and snap
concentrate flavor and thicken via evaporation
helps inhibit pathogens and maintain color
items.explanation: This concept is defined as: measure of free water available for microbes.

items.a: stabilize cocoa butter crystals for gloss and snap

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: denaturation of proteins.
items.a: protein structure unfolds due to heat, acid, or agitation

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: smoke point meaning.
items.a: temperature at which fat begins to visibly smoke and degrade
items.explanation: Core explanation: temperature at which fat begins to visibly smoke and degrade.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: difference between stock and broth.
items.a: stock is simmered from bones; broth is from meat and seasoning
items.explanation: Core explanation: stock is simmered from bones; broth is from meat and seasoning.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: emulsion breaking in sauces.
items.explanation: Core explanation: fat and water phases separate.

items.q: Explain the culinary science concept: gelatinization in starch cooking.
items.a: starch granules absorb water and thicken when heated
items.explanation: Core explanation: starch granules absorb water and thicken when heated.

items.q: A broken emulsion means the sauce phases have separated.
